name: Grand Marnier Souffle Avec Creme Anglaise
recipeIngredient: ¼ cup Grand Marnier or Cointreau
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (175°C).
2. Butter and dust a 2-quart souffle dish with powdered sugar; set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, melt butter; stir in flour and cook for 1 minute.
4. Add milk and continuously stir over medium heat until thickened.
5. Cool slightly.
6. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolks with ¼ cup sugar and salt until thick.
7. Mix in the liqueur (Grand Marnier or Cointreau).
8. Stir the egg yolk mixture into the milk mixture.
9. Fold gently into the souffle dish. The soufflé can be prepared up to this point 2 hours before serving.
10. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with 1 tablespoon sugar until stiff.
11. Lightly fold the beaten egg whites into the soufflé batter.
12. Place in the preheated oven and bake for 30–35 minutes or until puffed and golden brown.
13. Serve immediately, accompanied by Creme Anglaise.
Creme Anglaise:
1. Heat cream in a saucepan until warm; do not boil.
2. In another saucepan, combine egg yolks, sugar, and salt, beating until light yellow in color.
3. Slowly add the hot cream to the egg yolk mixture while stirring continuously.
4. Cook over medium heat until the sauce thickens, stirring with a wooden spoon.
5. Remove from heat and let cool.
6. Stir in Grand Marnier just before serving.
